taxonomy: Rhopalocyclus weigangianus is a species of extinct millipede that has been identified in the fossil record. It belongs to the phylum Arthropoda, class Diplopoda, and order Sphaerotheriida. The genus Rhopalocyclus is thought to be part of a larger group called Sphaerotheriida, which is a group of millipedes characterized by their rounded bodies and short legs. The classification of Rhopalocyclus weigangianus is based on fossil evidence and morphological characteristics of the species. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of this species within the Sphaerotheriida order.
